message:
feat(refresh-ledger): evidence-grade derived metrics for stale-refresh tuning [coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.24]

ibuuh.24 SCOPE bullet 1: "Establish a hard evidence ledger for the
stale-refresh path so future tuning is grounded in measured truth."
The raw RefreshLedger captures phase counters and timings; benchmark
agents and operator dashboards still need *derived* summaries
(throughput, phase-share, hot-phase identification) that are stable
across runs and trivially comparable. This commit adds those
pure-data summaries.

What landed:
- pub struct RefreshThroughputProfile { phase, duration_ms,
  items_processed, items_per_second }: per-phase headline tuning
  metric. items_per_second is None on zero-duration / zero-items
  (no NaN poisoning of benchmark JSON).
- pub struct RefreshPhaseShare { phase, duration_ms, share_pct }:
  per-phase wall-clock share. Sums to ~100.0 across phases that ran
  (sub-millisecond rounding ±0.05 drift). Zero-duration ⇒ 0.0
  (not NaN).
- pub struct RefreshLedgerEvidence: single-shot summary with
  throughput vec (excludes zero-item phases), phase_share vec
  (includes ALL phases for transparent summing), dominant_phase
  pointer ("where to optimize next"), aggregate_items_processed,
  aggregate_duration_ms, aggregate_items_per_second.
- impl RefreshLedger::evidence_summary(): pure O(phases) computation
  with zero allocations beyond the output. No I/O.
- Helpers items_per_second_for() and share_pct_for() that codify
  the zero-duration/zero-items safety contract in one place.

Five new golden gates pin the math + degenerate-case behavior:
- evidence_summary_reports_per_phase_throughput_with_safe_zero_handling:
  mixed corpus (1000 items/500ms scan + 2000 items/1s persist + 0-item
  phases). Asserts throughput excludes zero-item phases, items/sec is
  exact (2000.0 + 2000.0), aggregate items/sec correct (1875.0).
- evidence_summary_handles_empty_and_zero_duration_ledgers: empty +
  instant runs MUST NOT panic, MUST NOT emit NaN, dominant_phase
  None, aggregate items/sec None. Pins the no-NaN contract directly.
- evidence_summary_phase_share_sums_to_one_hundred_and_dominant_phase_picks_max:
  shares sum to 100.0 ±0.05 (rounding tolerance), Persist's 60%
  share computed exactly, dominant_phase identifies the largest.
- evidence_summary_dominant_phase_tie_break_is_first_in_pipeline_order:
  pins the LAST-wins tie-break semantics from Iterator::max_by_key
  so a future change to first-vs-last fails the test (operators
  reading dominant_phase rely on stable ordering).
- evidence_summary_serializes_to_stable_json_field_set: pins serde
  field set + JSON round-trip values so a regression dropping a
  field (e.g. aggregate_items_per_second) trips immediately.

21/21 lib indexer::refresh_ledger tests green via
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p2 cargo test
--lib indexer::refresh_ledger.

This slice does not modify the existing RefreshLedger / PhaseRecord
contracts; downstream callers continue to work unchanged. Future
ibuuh.24 slices wire the evidence summary into the refresh-ledger
publication path (currently writes the raw ledger; will also emit
the evidence summary) and into bench/regression gates that compare
runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
